title: RRB NTPC Graduate Level Admit Card 2026 Out – Download Here
content:
RRB NTPC Graduate Level Admit Card 2026 Out – Download Here

Introduction

The RRB NTPC Graduate Level Admit Card 2026 has been released for candidates appearing in the Non-Technical Popular Categories (NTPC) recruitment examination. Applicants who successfully completed the application process can now download their hall ticket from the official Railway Recruitment Board login portal.

The admit card is an important document required for entry into the examination centre. It contains essential details such as the exam date, exam centre, reporting time, and important guidelines that candidates must follow during the exam. The Computer-Based Test (CBT-1) for the graduate-level NTPC posts is scheduled to be conducted between 16 March and 27 March 2026 across multiple examination centres in India.

RRB NTPC Graduate Level Admit Card 2026 Overview

Particulars Details

Exam Name RRB NTPC Graduate Level Examination 2026

Organization Name Railway Recruitment Board (RRB)

Post Category Non-Technical Popular Categories (Graduate Level)

Admit Card Status Released

Admit Card Release Date March 2026

CBT-1 Exam Date 16 March – 27 March 2026

Official Website https://indianrailways.gov.in

The Railway Recruitment Board conducts the NTPC examination to recruit candidates for various graduate-level posts such as Station Master, Goods Train Manager, and Senior Clerk in the Indian Railways recruitment system.

RRB NTPC Graduate Level Admit Card 2026 – Quick Information

Release Date: 12 March 2026

Exam Date: 16 March to 27 March 2026

Download Status: Active Now

Official Website: rrb.digialm.com / regional RRB websites

Total Vacancies: 5810 Posts (Graduate Level)

Advertisement No.: CEN 06/2025

RRB NTPC Admit Card Details

The admit card serves as the official permission letter allowing candidates to appear in the examination. Without the hall ticket, candidates will not be permitted to enter the exam centre.

Candidates should download their admit card well before the examination date to avoid any technical issues or last-minute problems. After downloading the document, applicants should check all the details printed on the hall ticket carefully.

The admit card usually includes the exam shift, reporting time, exam venue, and other instructions related to the CBT examination. It also confirms the candidate’s registration and eligibility to appear for the exam.

If any incorrect information is found on the admit card, candidates should immediately contact the respective Railway Recruitment Board for correction before the examination date.

Details Mentioned on RRB NTPC Admit Card

Candidates should verify the following information after downloading the admit card:

Candidate Name

Roll Number / Registration Number

Exam Date

Exam Shift and Reporting Time

Exam Centre Name and Address

Candidate Photograph and Signature

Important Instructions for the Examination

Ensuring the accuracy of these details is important for smooth entry and verification at the exam centre.
author: Sukhamay
